title: Lomelosia olgae

This family comprises around 320 species of plants, most of which are found in the Northern Hemisphere. Lomelosia olgae, in particular, is a species within the genus Lomelosia. The genus Lomelosia is a group of plants that are native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Eastern Europe. intro: Lomelosia olgae, also known as Althaea olgae, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, wounds, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of Lomelosia olgae include flavonoids, glycosides, and mucilages, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Lomelosia olgae is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used as a component in some commercial herbal supplements and creams.
